html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 携弹走荆棘中 - Describes the action of carrying a bow and walking through thorny bushes.
  • 顾谓人曰 - Means 'turning to say to someone.'
  • 此地当出天子 - A prophetic statement meaning 'this place will produce an emperor.'
  • 吏批其颊 - Means 'a clerk slapped his cheek.'
  • 汝是何人敢至此 - A question meaning 'who are you to dare come here?'
  • 宋州官家遣我去擒见宰相范质 - A statement meaning 'the official from Song Prefecture sent me to capture and present to Chancellor Fan Zhongyan.'
  • 病心耳 - Means 'it's just a mental illness.'
  • 安足问 - Means 'there's no need to ask.'
  • 遂叱去 - Means 'then he shouted at him to go away.'
  • 果自归德军节度使受禅 - Means 'indeed, he became the military governor of Guide Army and received the imperial throne.'
  • 遂升宋州为应天府 - Means 'then Song Prefecture was elevated to the status of Ying Tianfu.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太祖 - Refers to Emperor Taizu of Song (Zhao Kuangyin).
  • 周 - The Later Zhou dynasty, a short-lived dynasty preceding the Song dynasty.
  • 宋州 - An ancient prefecture in modern-day Shandong province.
  • 归德军 - A military district in the region.
  • 应天府 - The capital of the Song dynasty, located in modern-day Kaifeng.
  • 南郡 - A historical name for the region, which was later known as Nan Jun.
  • 范质 - A prominent official during the Later Zhou dynasty, who served as chancellor.

Proper Nouns

  • 太祖 - Emperor Taizu of Song (Zhao Kuangyin).
  • 周 - The Later Zhou dynasty.
  • 宋州 - An ancient prefecture in modern-day Shandong province.
  • 归德军 - A military district in the region.
  • 应天府 - The capital of the Song dynasty, located in modern-day Kaifeng.
  • 南郡 - A historical name for the region, which was later known as Nan Jun.
  • 范质 - A prominent official during the Later Zhou dynasty, who served as chancellor.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 开宝中 - Refers to the period during the reign of Emperor Taizu of Song (960-976 AD).
  • 自称玄天大圣玉帝辅臣 - Literally means 'self-proclaimed as the Holy Great Saint of the Black Heaven, the Assistant Minister of the Jade Emperor.'
  • 其声婴儿，历可辨 - Describes the voice as that of an infant, yet clear and distinguishable.
  • 远近之民祷祠者旁午 - Indicates that people from near and far were praying and offering sacrifices in large numbers.
  • 设醮于宫廷 - Refers to setting up a ritual ceremony in the imperial palace.
  • 天上宫阙成，玉锁开 - Describes the completion of heavenly palaces and the opening of the jade lock.
  • 十月二十日陛下当归天 - Predicts that on the twentieth day of the tenth month, the emperor will ascend to heaven.
  • 死固不惮 - Means 'death is not feared.'
  • 所恨者幽并未并 - Expresses regret over the fact that the regions of You and Wei have not been conquered.
  • 乞延三数年 - Requests an extension of three or more years.
  • 俟克复二州，去亦未晚 - Means 'until the two states are conquered, it is not too late to leave.'
  • 晋王有仁心，历数攸属 - Suggests that Prince Jin has a compassionate heart and is destined for the throne.
  • 陛下在天，亦自有位 - Indicates that the emperor will have a place in heaven.
  • 命系于左军 - Orders the spirit to be confined in the left military camp.
  • 将无验而罪焉 - Plans to punish if there is no verification.
  • 事符神告 - The events correspond to the spirit's prediction.
  • 度守真为道士 - Appoints Zhang Shouzhen as a Taoist priest.
  • 仍赐紫袍 - Grants him a purple robe, a symbol of high rank.
  • 遂营庙于之太平镇 - Builds a temple in Taiping Town, .
  • 神位次序，殿庑规模，一由神授 - The arrangement of the deities and the structure of the temple are all determined by the spirit.
  • 仍尊黑杀，号为翊圣 - Honors the Black Killing Spirit and gives it the title 'Yi Sheng.'
  • 至仁宗朝，追谥守真为传真大法师 - During the reign of Emperor Renzong, posthumously bestows the title 'Fa Zhen Da Fa Shi' (Great Master of Transmitting the True Teachings) on Zhang Shouzhen.
  • 事见《翊圣别传》 - The story is recorded in the 'Yi Sheng Bie Zhuan' (Biography of Yi Sheng).

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开宝 - The era name of Emperor Taizu of Song, indicating the time period.
  • 玄天大圣玉帝辅臣 - Refers to a deity associated with the Black Heaven and the Jade Emperor, a common figure in Taoist and folk religion.
  • 玉锁 - A metaphorical reference to the celestial gates or barriers.
  • 晋王 - Refers to Prince Jin, who later became Emperor Taizong of Song.
  • 紫袍 - A purple robe, a symbol of high rank and honor in Chinese culture.
  • 翊圣 - The title given to the Black Killing Spirit, indicating its role in protecting and guiding.
  • 传真大法师 - A posthumous title given to Zhang Shouzhen, indicating his importance in religious and cultural history.

Proper Nouns

  • 开宝 - The era name of Emperor Taizu of Song.
  • 凤翔府 - Fengxiang Prefecture, a region in Shaanxi Province.
  • 张守真 - Zhang Shouzhen, a commoner who became a Taoist priest.
  • 玄天大圣玉帝辅臣 - A deity associated with the Black Heaven and the Jade Emperor.
  • 晋王 - Prince Jin, who later became Emperor Taizong of Song.
  • 紫袍 - A purple robe, a symbol of high rank.
  • 翊圣 - The title given to the Black Killing Spirit.
  • 传真大法师 - A posthumous title given to Zhang Shouzhen.
  • 《翊圣别传》 - The biography of Yi Sheng.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 伏谒 - Formal act of kneeling and paying respects, indicating deep reverence.
  • 慰藉之甚厚 - Means 'consoled him greatly' or 'showed him great kindness.'
  • 入幕 - Means 'to enter the tent,' a metaphor for joining someone's inner circle or becoming a close advisor.
  • 顿首祈免 - Means 'kneeling and begging for exemption,' indicating a plea for mercy.
  • 甲观之庆 - Refers to a grand celebration or auspicious event, often associated with the birth of a royal heir.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 杨公砺 - Historical figure, likely a prominent official during the Song Dynasty.
  • 章圣皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Zhenzong of Song, known as 'Zhang Sheng Emperor.'
  • 皇太子 - Refers to the Crown Prince, the heir apparent to the throne.
  • 神文诞圣 - Refers to the birth of Emperor Shenzong of Song, who later became a significant ruler.
  • 南岳赤脚李仙人 - Refers to a Taoist immortal associated with Mount Heng in Hunan, often depicted as a barefoot figure who enjoys drinking.
  • 安退处士刘易 - Refers to Liu Yi, a reclusive scholar who lived during the Song Dynasty and recorded this story.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨公砺 - Yang Gong Li, a historical figure.
  • 章圣皇帝 - Emperor Zhenzong of Song.
  • 皇太子 - The Crown Prince, later Emperor Renzong of Song.
  • 神文 - A reference to Emperor Shenzong of Song.
  • 乐学士史 - Shi, a scholar-official.
  • 南岳赤脚李仙人 - A Taoist immortal associated with Mount Heng.
  • 安退处士刘易 - Liu Yi, a reclusive scholar.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秩满 - The term of office ended.
  • 考绩于吏部 - Had his performance evaluated by the Ministry of Personnel.
  • 引对 - To be summoned for an audience.
  • 便殿 - A smaller, more informal hall in the palace.
  • 黼座 - The throne, often decorated with a pattern of black and white.
  • 幼冲 - A respectful term for a young ruler, emphasizing their youth and inexperience.
  • 取旨 - To seek the emperor's will or approval.
  • 不豫 - A euphemism for illness, especially when referring to the emperor.
  • 监国 - To act as regent.
  • 睿姿 - The appearance of a wise and capable ruler.
  • 一如所梦 - Just as he had dreamed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天章阁 - The Tianzhang Pavilion, an imperial institution in the Song Dynasty.
  • 司马公池 - Sima Gongchi, a real historical figure who served as a supervisor in the Tianzhang Pavilion during the Song Dynasty.
  • 光山县 - Guangshan County, a county in the Henan Province during the Song Dynasty.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel, one of the six ministries in the Song Dynasty, responsible for evaluating officials' performance and promoting them.
  • 章圣 - A temple name for Emperor Zhenzong of Song, who reigned from 997 to 1022.
  • 资善堂 - Another name for the Zisha Hall, where the emperor held audiences with officials.
  • 神文 - Another name for Emperor Shenzong of Song, who reigned from 1067 to 1085.
  • 睿姿 - Refers to the appearance of a wise and capable ruler, often used to describe the emperor's demeanor.

Proper Nouns

  • 天章阁 - The Tianzhang Pavilion.
  • 司马公池 - Sima Gongchi.
  • 光山县 - Guangshan County.
  • 吏部 - The Ministry of Personnel.
  • 章圣 - Emperor Zhenzong of Song.
  • 便殿 - A small hall in the palace.
  • 黼座 - The throne.
  • 幼冲 - A respectful term for a young ruler.
  • 神文 - Emperor Shenzong of Song.
  • 资善堂 - Another name for the Zisha Hall.
  • 睿姿 - The appearance of a wise and capable ruler.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 干事既畢 - Having completed his affairs; indicates the monk had finished his tasks.
  • 秘之，勿妄言 - Keep it secret, do not speak of it lightly; advice given by the county magistrate.
  • 新天子即位，南岳神往受职耳 - The new emperor has ascended the throne, and the god of Mount Heng is going to receive his official position; explains the reason for the procession in the dream.
  • 此四海之神洎河北来受职也 - These are the gods of the four seas and the north coming to receive their positions; statement about the supernatural beings.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉八年三月 - The third month of the eighth year of the Jia You era; specific date in the Chinese calendar.
  • 衡山县 - Hengshan County; located in Hunan Province, China.
  • 衡山 - Mount Heng; one of the Five Great Mountains of China, associated with Taoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 南岳神 - The god of Mount Heng; significant deity in Chinese mythology.
  • 仁宗 - Emperor Renzong of Song; notable emperor known for his cultural and political reforms.
  • 太公 - Duke Tai Gong (Jiang Ziya); legendary figure in Chinese history, known for his wisdom and contributions to the establishment of the Zhou Dynasty.
  • 四海之神 - The gods of the four seas; important deities in Chinese mythology, representing the four cardinal directions.
  • 河北 - The northern region of China; often associated with the Yellow River.
  • 金匮 - The *Jin Gui* (Golden Treasury); historical text that records various legends and stories.

Proper Nouns

  • 嘉八年三月 - The third month of the eighth year of the Jia You era.
  • 衡山县 - Hengshan County.
  • 衡山 - Mount Heng.
  • 南岳神 - The god of Mount Heng.
  • 仁宗 - Emperor Renzong of Song.
  • 太公 - Duke Tai Gong (Jiang Ziya).
  • 四海之神 - The gods of the four seas.
  • 河北 - The northern region of China.
  • 金匮 - The *Jin Gui* (Golden Treasury).

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南岳真人 - Revered figure associated with Mount Heng (Nanyue), a sacred site in Chinese Daoism; '真人' means 'True Person' or 'Immortal.'
  • 致政 - Means 'to retire from office' or 'to resign from a position of power.'
  • 被疾 - Means 'to suffer from illness.'
  • 病革 - Means 'to be critically ill.'
  • 奄然 - Means 'suddenly' or 'abruptly,' often used to describe sudden death.
  • 屏左右 - Means 'to dismiss attendants' or 'to be alone.'
  • 手书密封 - Means 'to write by hand and seal it,' indicating a personal and confidential message.
  • 恍惚 - Means 'confused' or 'unsteady,' often used to describe a state of mind.
  • 久病恍惚 - Means 'long-term illness causing confusion.'
  • 上仙 - Refers to the death of an emperor, specifically the ascension to the afterlife or heaven.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 南岳真人 - Reference to a Daoist sage associated with Mount Heng, one of the Five Great Mountains in China and a significant site in Daoist tradition.
  • 仁宗皇帝 - Refers to Emperor Renzong of Song (1022–1063), the fourth emperor of the Song dynasty, known for his benevolent rule and cultural patronage.
  • 玉皇 - Refers to the Jade Emperor, the supreme deity in Chinese folk religion and Taoism, often depicted as the ruler of the heavens.
  • 朝玉皇 - Means 'to pay homage to the Jade Emperor,' indicating a ritual or ceremonial act.
  • 卷帘毕 - Means 'after the curtain was drawn,' a reference to the opening of a ceremonial space.

Proper Nouns

  • 南岳真人 - The revered figure associated with Mount Heng.
  • 仁宗皇帝 - Emperor Renzong of Song.
  • 玉皇 - The Jade Emperor.
  • 南岳 - Mount Heng, one of the Five Great Mountains in China.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 會聖宮 - A temple dedicated to the worship of ancestral spirits.
  • 羽衛 - Ceremonial guards or entourage of a monarch.
  • 赭袍 - A type of robe, often associated with royalty or high officials.
  • 黃蓋 - A yellow canopy or umbrella, symbolizing imperial authority.
  • 仁廟上仙 - A euphemism for the death of Emperor Renzong.
  • 天聖末洎明道中 - Refers to the late Tiansheng and early Mingdao periods of Emperor Renzong's reign.
  • 京師市井坊巷之人 - Common people of the capital city.
  • 曹門好 - A colloquial expression meaning 'good from Caowei,' likely referring to a place or district known for its quality.
  • 曹門高 - A colloquial expression meaning 'tall or grand from Caowei,' possibly referring to something impressive.
  • 神文 - A respectful term for Emperor Renzong, emphasizing his wise and just rule.
  • 曹王女孫 - Refers to the granddaughter of the Prince of Cao.
  • 國功臣之冠 - Means 'the foremost among national heroes.'
  • 圭爵蟬聯 - Refers to the continuous succession of high-ranking officials or nobles.
  • 作配宸極 - Means 'to be matched to the imperial throne,' indicating a marriage to the emperor.
  • 外戚之尊 - Refers to the high status of an imperial relative by marriage.
  • 王輔藝祖 - Refers to the assistance provided by the ancestors of the Wang family in establishing the Song dynasty.
  • 降蜀平吳 - Refers to the conquest of Shu (Shu Han) and Wu (Eastern Wu) during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 抗丑虜 - Means 'to resist barbarian enemies.'
  • 破強敵 - Means 'to defeat powerful foes.'
  • 將百萬之眾 - Means 'to lead a million troops.'
  • 妄殺一人 - Means 'to kill one person unjustly.'
  • 後裔之興 - Refers to the prosperity of the descendants.
  • 唐郭尚父 - Refers to Guo Ziyi, a prominent general of the Tang dynasty.
  • 圭爵蟬聯者三世 - Means 'the continuous succession of high-ranking officials for three generations.'
  • 作配宸極 - Means 'to be matched to the imperial throne.'
  • 居母后之尊 - Refers to the high status of an empress dowager.
  • 人君行子孙之礼 - Means 'the emperor performs the rites of a son.'
  • 社稷之功未泯 - Means 'the merit of founding the state has not diminished.'
  • 復鐘慶於懿安 - Means 'to bestow blessings on the Empress E'anan.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 會聖宮 - A temple in Luoyang dedicated to the worship of ancestral spirits.
  • 羽衛 - Ceremonial guards or entourage of a monarch.
  • 赭袍 - A type of robe, often associated with royalty or high officials.
  • 黄盖 - A yellow canopy or umbrella, symbolizing imperial authority.
  • 仁庙上仙 - A euphemism for the death of Emperor Renzong.
  • 天圣末洎明道中 - Refers to the late Tiansheng and early Mingdao periods of Emperor Renzong's reign.
  • 京师市井坊巷之人 - Common people of the capital city.
  • 曹门好 - A colloquial expression indicating something of high quality from Caowei.
  • 曹门高 - A colloquial expression indicating something impressive from Caowei.
  • 神文 - A respectful term for Emperor Renzong.
  • 曹王女孙 - Granddaughter of the Prince of Cao.
  • 国功臣之冠 - The foremost among national heroes.
  • 圭爵蝉联 - Continuous succession of high-ranking officials or nobles.
  • 作配宸极 - Marriage to the emperor.
  • 外戚之尊 - High status of an imperial relative by marriage.
  • 王辅艺祖 - Assistance provided by the ancestors of the Wang family in establishing the Song dynasty.
  • 降蜀平吴 - Conquest of Shu and Wu during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 抗丑虏 - Resistance against barbarian enemies.
  • 破强敌 - Defeat of powerful foes.
  • 将百万之众 - Leading a million troops.
  • 妄杀一人 - Unjust killing.
  • 后裔之兴 - Prosperity of descendants.
  • 唐郭尚父 - Guo Ziyi, a prominent general of the Tang dynasty.
  • 圭爵蝉联者三世 - Continuous succession of high-ranking officials for three generations.
  • 作配宸极 - Marriage to the emperor.
  • 居母后之尊 - High status of an empress dowager.
  • 人君行子孙之礼 - Emperor performing the rites of a son.
  • 社稷之功未泯 - Merit of founding the state has not diminished.
  • 复钟庆于懿安 - Bestowing blessings on the Empress E'anan.

Proper Nouns

  • 會聖宮 - The temple dedicated to ancestral spirits.
  • 洛都 - Luoyang, the capital city.
  • 望仙橋 - A bridge in Luoyang.
  • 仁廟 - The temple dedicated to Emperor Renzong.
  • 上仙 - A euphemism for death.
  • 天聖末 - The late Tiansheng period of Emperor Renzong's reign.
  • 明道中 - The early Mingdao period of Emperor Renzong's reign.
  • 京師市井坊巷之人 - Common people of the capital city.
  • 曹門 - A place or district known for its quality.
  • 神文 - A respectful term for Emperor Renzong.
  • 曹王 - The Prince of Cao.
  • 國功臣之冠 - The foremost among national heroes.
  • 圭爵蟬聯 - Continuous succession of high-ranking officials or nobles.

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 倜傥有气节 - Describes a person who is bold, unrestrained, and has a strong sense of integrity.
  • 通《诗》《易》 - Indicates proficiency in the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry) and *Yijing* (Book of Changes).
  • 以进士特奏名得三礼出身 - Refers to obtaining a degree in the Three Rituals through a special imperial examination.
  • 荐为邑佐 - Means being recommended for a local administrative position.
  • 有能声 - Indicates a reputation for ability or competence.
  • 以公事忤郡太守 - Means offending the prefect with official matters.
  • 辄致所事而去 - Means immediately resigning from the position.
  • 僦舟东下 - Means hiring a boat to travel eastward.
  • 隐于叶山 - Means hiding or retreating to Yeshan.
  • 以太子中舍致仕 - Means retiring from official service as a scholar in the Imperial Academy.
  • 值岁荒 - Means encountering a year of famine.
  • 徙家京师 - Means moving the family to the capital.
  • 卖药自给 - Means selling medicine to support oneself.
  • 踵门者辄避去 - Means those who visit are immediately avoided.
  • 遗金帛 - Means leaving behind money and silk.
  • 即散道士丐者 - Means immediately distributing the money to Taoist priests and beggars.
  • 未尝有所蓄 - Means never accumulating anything.
  • 与其妻孔氏皆学辟谷 - Means both he and his wife, Kong, learned the practice of abstaining from food.
  • 往往经岁不食 - Means sometimes going for a whole year without eating.
  • 思武陵山水之嘉 - Means thinking the landscape of Wuling is beautiful.
  • 尽室出彼 - Means moving the entire family to Wuling.
  • 王介甫高其行 - Means Wang Anshi admired his conduct.
  • 以诗送 - Means sending a poem as a farewell.
  • 知君欲上武陵溪，水自东流人自西 - A poetic expression indicating Wang Anshi's admiration and the idea that the poet is going to a place where the flow of life is opposite to his own.
  • 既至武陵 - Means after arriving in Wuling.
  • 结庐于高梧市 - Means building a hut in Gao Wu Market.
  • 丧其妻 - Means losing his wife.
  • 自是不接人事 - Means no longer interacting with people.
  • 杜门称疾 - Means closing the door and pretending to be ill.
  • 惟焚香诵《易》而已 - Means only burning incense and reciting the *Yijing*.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陈靖 - A historical figure from the Song Dynasty, known for his moral integrity and reclusive lifestyle.
  • 范讽 - Fan Feng, a contemporary and friend.
  • 石延年 - Shi Yanian, a contemporary and friend.
  • 刘潜 - Liu Qian, a contemporary and friend.
  • 太子中舍 - A position in the Imperial Academy.
  • 致仕 - The act of retiring from official service.
  • 岁荒 - A year of famine.
  • 京师 - The capital city, which was Bianjing (modern-day Kaifeng) during the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 王介甫 - Wang Anshi, a prominent statesman and poet of the Northern Song Dynasty.
  • 武陵 - A region in Hunan Province known for its scenic beauty.
  • 高梧市 - A market in Wuling.
  • 《易》 - The *Yijing* (Book of Changes), one of the Five Classics.

Proper Nouns

  • 陈靖 - Chen Jing, the subject of the passage.
  • 范讽 - Fan Feng, a contemporary and friend.
  • 石延年 - Shi Yanian, a contemporary and friend.
  • 刘潜 - Liu Qian, a contemporary and friend.
  • 太子中舍 - A position in the Imperial Academy.
  • 京师 - The capital city, Bianjing (modern-day Kaifeng).
  • 王介甫 - Wang Anshi, a prominent statesman and poet.
  • 武陵 - A region in Hunan Province.
  • 高梧市 - A market in Wuling.
  • 《易》 - The *Yijing* (Book of Changes).

[9]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 六年七月十七日亭午 - A precise date and time in Classical Chinese, '六年' means the sixth year of a reign, '七月十七日' is the seventeenth day of the seventh month, and '亭午' means noon.
  • 遽命其子庠具紙札 - '遽命' means 'hurriedly ordered,' '其子庠' refers to his son named Xiang, and '具紙札' means 'to prepare paper and writing materials.'
  • 作書遺張郎中曰 - '作書' means 'to write a letter,' '遺' means 'to send,' and '張郎中' is the name of the recipient, Zhang Langzhong Zhi.
  • 近上帝以靖平生無谄，俾主判地下平直司 - '近上帝' means 'recently by the Heavenly Emperor,' '以靖平生無谄' means 'because of Jing's lifelong integrity and lack of flattery,' '俾主判' means 'has appointed him to judge,' and '地下平直司' refers to the 'Underworld Justice Department.'
  • 候天符下，即之任矣 - '候天符下' means 'awaiting the heavenly decree,' and '即之任矣' means 'to immediately assume the position.'
  • 得書以靖為病心者，不复报 - '得书' means 'received the letter,' '以靖为病心者' means 'thinking that Jing was suffering from a mental illness,' and '不复报' means 'did not respond.'
  • 自是多为歌诗，皆有脱去世俗之意 - '自是' means 'from then on,' '多为歌诗' means 'often wrote poems,' and '皆有脱去世俗之意' means 'all expressed a desire to transcend the mundane world.'
  • 七年十一月十二日平旦 - '七年' means the seventh year, '十一月十二日' is the twelfth day of the eleventh month, and '平旦' means early morning.
  • 谓其子曰 - '谓' means 'told,' and '其子' means 'his son.'
  • 吾数尽矣，后事一托张秘丞主之 - '吾数尽矣' means 'my time is up,' '后事' means 'after-death affairs,' and '一托张秘丞主之' means 'entrust everything to Zhang, the Secretariat Commissioner.'
  • 言讫而终 - '言讫' means 'after finishing speaking,' and '而终' means 'he died.'

  • 其妻洎孙如期而死 - 其妻 means 'his wife,' 洎孙 means 'and his grandson,' 如期 means 'as scheduled,' and 而死 means 'died.' This phrase means 'his wife and grandson died as scheduled.'

  • 与刁居密迩 - 与 means 'and,' 刁居 means 'Dao's house,' and 密迩 means 'very close.' This phrase means 'and very close to Dao's house.'

  • 惟刁宅独完 - 惟 means 'only,' 刁宅 means 'Dao's house,' and 独完 means 'alone remained intact.' This phrase means 'only Dao's house remained intact.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '有丐者死于汴河，岸之侧有败席短杖' describes a beggar who died near the Bian River, with rotten mats and short sticks nearby.
  • The sentence '时方大雪，独不积其身' describes a heavy snowfall, but the beggar's body was not covered in snow, which is unusual.
  • The sentence '麦异之，为市衫裤麻履故巾，瘗之于隙地' describes the eunuch's reaction to the beggar's unusual state and his subsequent actions.
  • The sentence '他日奉使薄坂北一邮置，有一贫人诣门请见，仍云：“尝受恩，故来致谢。”' describes the eunuch's encounter with a poor man who claims to have received a favor.
  • The sentence '麦召见，询其由，曰：“自顶至踵皆君所赐也。”' explains that the poor man claims everything from head to toe was given by the eunuch.
  • The sentence '麦罔然，良久方省瘗丐者事，乃延坐与语，屏左右，移时而去' describes the eunuch's confusion and eventual recall of burying the beggar, followed by a private conversation.
  • The sentence '麦既回京，发瘗，但见席杖而已' describes the eunuch's discovery upon returning to the capital.
  • The sentence '麦遂弃官为道士，为左街道录，年九十余卒' describes the eunuch's decision to resign from his official position and become a Taoist priest, living to be over ninety years old.

Proper Nouns

  • 薄坂北一邮置 - A post station to the north of the thin slope.
  • 左街道录 - A title for a Taoist priest in the Left Street.

  • 尋索鑑熟視 - Phrase combining actions of searching and examining, meaning 'Searched for and examined the mirror closely.'
  • 隨手而碎 - Vivid description of the mirror breaking easily, meaning 'Broke it with a touch.'
  • 窮極微旨 - Suggests a deep and thorough understanding, meaning 'To exhaustively explore the subtle meanings.'
  • 傳信於永世也 - Suggests the intention to preserve and pass down the story, meaning 'To transmit credibility to future generations.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '弃妻子，游名山，数年不归' describes a sequence of actions: abandoning family, wandering in famous mountains, and not returning for several years.
  • The sentence '方道旧为乐而酒薄不可饮' is somewhat elliptical, but it conveys that the joy of reminiscing is marred by the poor quality of the wine.
  • The phrase '夜漏上四鼓，诸公皆酩酊就寝' describes a sequence of events: it was late at night, and all the guests were drunk and went to bed.
  • The conditional clause '或诸公睡起，报云我且归真定也' can be interpreted as a future action if the guests wake up.
  • The phrase '静今隐嵩少间，岁或一至、或再至' describes the current state of Jing and his occasional visits.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 业进士 - Meaning 'pursued the path of a jinshi (imperial scholar).'
  • 其父前妻生三子而亡 - Describes the death of Zhang Han's father's first wife, who gave birth to three sons.
  • 父再娶窦氏 - Indicates that Zhang Han's father remarried a woman from the Dòu family.
  • 翰窦出也 - Clarifies that Zhang Han was born to the Dòu wife.
  • 窦之生，岁月日时不利于夫 - Suggests that the Dòu wife's birth date was considered inauspicious for her husband.
  • 遂减岁，迁就吉辰 - Describes the practice of adjusting the birth date to a more favorable one.
  • 而归于张氏 - Indicates that the Dòu wife was brought into the Zhang family.
  • 间与厥夫祷嗣于归真观之三清殿 - Describes the couple's practice of praying for offspring at the Sanqing Hall in the Guizhen Temple.
  • 祝辞以所减之齿告焉 - Indicates that the prayer included the adjusted birth date.
  • 继育数子 - Describes the subsequent births of more children.
  • 而翰父物故 - Euphemism for 'died,' indicating Zhang Han's father passed away.
  • 会归真观火 - Describes the event where the Guizhen Temple caught fire.
  • 窦密以镪五十万与道士修殿宇 - Indicates that the Dòu wife secretly gave 500,000 units of money to the Taoist priests to repair the temple.
  • 少时窦亦死 - Describes the Dòu wife's death after a short time.
  • 后数岁，翰忽为神所凭 - Describes an event where Zhang Han was suddenly possessed by a deity.
  • 以手执髻，鞠躬曰 - Describes Zhang Han's physical and verbal actions during the possession.
  • 听圣语 - Indicates that the deity spoke through Zhang Han.
  • 窦氏以诈伪之岁诬罔上真 - Critiques the Dòu wife for lying about her birth date to deceive the true deity.
  • 又弗询于子 - Indicates that the Dòu wife did not inform her children about the deception.
  • 私用家赀 - Describes the Dòu wife's secret use of family wealth.
  • 已受考于阴府 - Suggests that the Dòu wife has been judged in the underworld.
  • 今则为异类矣 - Indicates that the Dòu wife has been transformed into a supernatural entity.
  • 事皆秘密，众所不知者 - Emphasizes the secrecy of the events.
  • 由是荆人率闻之 - Indicates that people from Jing generally became aware of the events.
  • 噫 - Interjection expressing disappointment or regret.
  • 女子增减其年以利适人者 - Describes the practice of adjusting a woman's age to improve her marriage prospects.
  • 为过虽小，妄以告神则罪大也 - Critiques the act of lying to deities, even if the mistake is small.
  • 专取家帑以用构祠堂 - Describes the Dòu wife's use of family wealth to build a shrine.
  • 不俾子知 - Indicates that the Dòu wife did not tell her children about the shrine construction.
  • 神尚责怒 - Suggests that the deity still holds a grudge.
  • 矧非理而用者乎 - Emphasizes the severity of using family wealth unjustly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 张翰 - Zhang Han, a historical figure from Jiangling, is the subject of the text. He is described as a scholar who pursued the jinshi degree.
  • 江陵 - Jiangling, a city in present-day Hubei Province, China, is mentioned as Zhang Han's place of origin.
  • 进士 - The highest degree in the imperial examination system, indicating Zhang Han's scholarly pursuits.
  • 归真观 - Guizhen Temple, a Taoist temple, is mentioned as a place where Zhang Han's parents prayed for offspring.
  • 三清殿 - Sanqing Hall, a hall within the Guizhen Temple dedicated to the Three Pure Ones, the highest deities in Taoism.
  • 阴府 - The underworld, where souls are judged after death, is mentioned in the context of the Dòu wife's punishment.
  • 荆人 - People from Jing (Jiangling), indicating the local audience for the story.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兩舉進士不預荐送 - Failed to be recommended twice for the jinshi examination. '不预荐送' means the candidate was not recommended by local authorities for the examination.
  • 改業明法 - Changed his career to study legal matters. '改業' means 'to change one's career or profession.'
  • 苟得入仕 - If I can enter officialdom. '苟得' is idiomatic, meaning 'if one can manage to' or 'if one can somehow.'
  • 我诵法令 - I recite legal codes. '诵法令' is a direct reference to studying and memorizing legal texts.
  • 顶有戴笄 - There is a hairpin on the top of his head. '戴笄' refers to a hairpin, traditionally worn by women, but here used metaphorically to describe a physical mark.
  • 颈有投缳之痕 - There is a mark of a hanging on his neck. '投缳' is an idiomatic expression for hanging oneself, and '之痕' means 'the mark of.'
  • 屡与之饮 - Often drank with him. '屡与之饮' is a casual way of saying 'often drank with him.'
  • 数爵之后 - After several cups. '数爵' refers to the number of cups of wine, and '之后' means 'afterward.'
  • 授潞州潞城县令 - Was appointed as the magistrate of Lucheng County in Lü Prefecture. '授' means 'to appoint' or 'to grant a position.'
  • 到任而终 - Died upon taking office. '到任' means 'upon taking office,' and '而终' means 'and died.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 进士 - Refers to the highest level of the imperial examination system, the primary means of selecting government officials in imperial China.
  • 明法 - Refers to the study of legal codes and regulations, a specialized field of study in the imperial examination system.
  • 梦五色光 - A dream of five-colored light, often associated with divine or supernatural visions in Chinese literature.
  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist priest or monk, a supernatural figure providing guidance in this context.
  • 中年 - Refers to middle age, typically around 40-50 years old.
  • 令 - Refers to a county magistrate, a low-level but important administrative position in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 投缳 - An idiomatic expression for hanging oneself, a common method of suicide in traditional Chinese culture.

  • 童稚持鉴来治者 - Means 'children would bring mirrors to be polished,' indicating that even young people sought his services.
  • 遇醉则或抵破之 - Means 'when he was drunk, he would sometimes break them,' describing his erratic behavior when intoxicated.
  • 引之长三尺 - Means 'he would stretch them to three feet long,' indicating his playful manipulation of the mirrors.
  • 小儿惊呼 - Means 'the children would cry out in surprise,' describing the reaction of the children.

  • 未尝一日不醉 - Means 'he never went a day without being drunk,' indicating his habitual drunkenness.
  • 拂衣入王屋山 - Means 'he would put on his robe and enter Wangwu Mountain,' describing his departure.
  • 立而尸解于药柜山中 - Means 'he stood and underwent a transformation in the mountain of the medicine cabinet,' indicating his spiritual transformation.
  • 始村人见有人立于岩石之上 - Means 'at first, villagers saw someone standing on a rock,' describing the initial sighting.

  • 顶有一窍如鸡卵大 - Means 'there was a hole on his head as big as an egg,' describing the physical characteristic.

  • 果惊去矣 - Indeed, it has been startled away. '惊去' (startled away) is a common way to describe someone or something fleeing due to fear.
  • 贽纨素数匹以为寿 - Present several bolts of fine silk as a birthday gift. '贽' (present) is an archaic word for a gift, and '纨素' (fine silk) is a specific type of gift.
  • 若烟绡雾 - Like smoke and mist. This is a poetic description, comparing something to smoke and mist.
  • 蛛丝组织而成 - Woven from spider silk. This is a metaphorical description, suggesting something delicate and intricate.
  • 非女工之所能杼轴也 - Not something that can be done by a woman's needlework. '杼轴' (needlework) refers to traditional women's work.
  • 兄已授明越巡检 - My brother has been appointed as the inspector of Ming Yue. '巡检' (inspector) is an official title.
  • 明日宣下，令先兄往彼 - Tomorrow, it will be announced, and my elder brother will go there. '宣下' (announced) is a formal way to say something will be officially declared.

  • 恐知是亲戚，故起风涛相戏 - They might know we are related, so they play with wind and waves. '起风涛相戏' (play with wind and waves) is a metaphor for creating disturbances.
  • 不须惮也 - There is no need to fear. '惮' (fear) is used to express concern.
  • 言讫，即不见 - After speaking, he immediately disappeared. '言讫' (after speaking) marks the end of a statement.
  • 虹梁自室门而起 - A rainbow beam rises from the door of the room. '虹梁' (rainbow beam) is a poetic description.
  • 南望无际 - Looking south, there is no end. '无际' (no end) emphasizes vastness.

  • 果徙明越巡检 - Indeed, he was transferred to the inspector of Ming Yue. '徙' (transferred) indicates a change in position.
  • 将至任 - When he is about to take office. '将至任' (about to take office) describes the upcoming assumption of a new role.
  • 非敢故为异味 - Not daring to intentionally create strange flavors. '异味' (strange flavors) is a metaphor for unusual or unexpected things.

  • 与兄缘数已尽，从此辞矣 - The缘分 (fate) with my brother has come to an end, so I must bid farewell. '缘数' (fate) refers to the predetermined course of events.

  • 竟不知所适 - Finally, he does not know where he will go. '所适' (where he will go) is a question about his destination.
  • 蔡族亦无他咎 - The Cai family also has no other misfortune. '咎' (misfortune) refers to bad luck or calamity.
